channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Highlights From the 2026 Tony Awards
item.description: “Death of a Salesman,” “Liberation” and “Ragtime” won big at the 2026 Tony Awards, which celebrated the best of Broadway.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

item.title: Deadly Quake Hits Southern Philippines
item.description: A deadly 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck off the island of Mindanao in the southern Philippines, causing buildings to collapse and triggering tsunami warnings in the region.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang

item.title: Uno es el papa, el otro es ateo. Ambos se oponen a Trump
item.description: El papa León ha provocado la furia del presidente de EE. UU. por criticar la guerra en Irán y el presidente del gobierno español se ha opuesto a Trump en muchos temas. Sus motivaciones, sin embargo, parecen diferentes.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Vatican Media, vía Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: El papa León XIV recibiendo al presidente del gobierno español, Pedro Sánchez, en el Vaticano el mes pasado, en una foto facilitada por los medios de comunicación vaticanos.

item.title: Nikol Pashinyan Wins Re-election in Armenia
item.description: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan overcame a pressure campaign by Moscow and was on track to win a mandate to move ahead on peace talks that President Trump helped broker.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne

media:content.media:description: Nikol Pashinyan at a polling station in Yerevan, Armenia, on Sunday.

item.title: Live Updates: Iran and Israel Trade Strikes for the First Time Since April
item.description: Iran launched waves of missiles at Israel, while Israel struck military targets and a petrochemical complex in Iran.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Trump, Mamdani and the Knicks: A Manic Monday Awaits New York
item.description: When the Knicks return home for Game 3 of the N.B.A. finals, much of New York City will be watching, including some well-known guests.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich, Dana Rubinstein and William K. Rashbaum

media:content.media:credit: Shuran Huang for The New York Times

item.title: Inside the Subway Expansion a Century in the Making
item.description: The next phase of the Second Avenue subway, first proposed in the 1920s, could finally be completed by 2032, creating a new transit hub in Harlem.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen and Dave Sanders

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times

item.title: Young Knicks Fans Rejoice Over Their Team’s Run for the Ages
item.description: New Yorkers under 30 know about the past pain and suffering. But now they have reason to believe.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Yan, Jonah E. Bromwich and Jonah Rosenberg

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 8, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: 5 minutos para que te enamores de Charlie Parker
item.description: Bird ayudó a introducir el bebop, un estilo que transformó el jazz de una música popular para bailar en una forma de arte más exigente y exploratoria. He aquí 11 piezas para introducirte a su legado perdurable.
item.dc:creator: Marcus J. Moore

item.title: Word of the Day: supine
item.description: This word has appeared in 30 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: ‘Every Year After’ and 8 More Shows to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: The adaptation of a Carley Fortune novel airs and a new season of “Below Deck Mediterranean” begins.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

item.title: Putin Restricted the Internet. It Has Not Gone to Plan.
item.description: In destroying what remained of a relatively free internet, Vladimir Putin has broken a longstanding social contract.
item.dc:creator: Andrei Zakharov

item.title: Searching for Shade When It’s 125 Degrees
item.description: Every season brings a new struggle for people in Dadu District, Pakistan, an area prone to sandstorms, drought and flooding.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman and Asim Hafeez

media:content.media:credit: Asim Hafeez for The New York Times

item.title: No Corrections: June 8, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, June 8, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: In His Role 30 Years, A Last Note Draws Near
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, June 8, 2026.

item.title: A ‘Miraculous Transformation’: How Kim Jong-un Fortified North Korea
item.description: He used the pandemic to ruthlessly tighten his grip on the country. Then he energized its economy by leveraging Russia’s war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun

media:content.media:description: Kim Jong-un arriving at the Dong Dang railway station in Vietnam, near the Chinese border, in 2019.

item.title: One Is the Pope, the Other an Atheist. They Both Oppose Trump.
item.description: Pope Leo XIV and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of Spain, who meet on Monday, have recently clashed with President Trump. Their motivations, however, may be different.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Vatican Media, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV welcoming Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of Spain at the Vatican last month, in a photo made available by Vatican media.

item.title: ‘Schmigadoon!’ Wins Best Musical Tony Award
item.description: The knowing, and loving, musical theater satire won the coveted award of the night.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson

media:content.media:description: A scene from Broadway’s “Schmigadoon!,” which is running at the Nederlander Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: Joshua Henry Wins the Tony for Best Actor in a Musical.
item.description: As Coalhouse Walker Jr. in “Ragtime,” Henry belts out emotional solos that have prompted mid-show standing ovations.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Sherman

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Tullo for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Joshua Henry, who plays Coalhouse Walker Jr. in the revival of “Ragtime.”

item.title: 6 Wounded in Stabbing at New York Penn Station
item.description: A man whose identity has not been released was taken into custody by Amtrak police officers. All of the victims were expected to survive.
item.dc:creator: Chelsia Rose Marcius and Miles G. Cohen

media:content.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An Amtrak police officer spoke with a New York Police Department detective after a stabbing at Pennsylvania Station in Manhattan on Sunday.

item.title: ‘Death of a Salesman’ Wins the Tony for Best Revival of a Play
item.description: Arthur Miller’s classic won the same award in 1984, 1999 and 2012.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Green

media:content.media:description: Laurie Metcalf, left, as Linda and Nathan Lane as Willy Loman in the Arthur Miller play “Death of a Salesman” at the Winter Garden Theater in New York.

item.title: At Least 15 Dead After Strong Earthquake in Southern Philippines
item.description: The 7.8-magnitude quake struck off the island of Mindanao, toppling buildings and damaging bridges. Dozens were injured and tens of thousands displaced.
item.dc:creator: Jason Gutierrez, Aie Balagtas See and Sui-Lee Wee

media:content.media:description: A partially collapsed shopping plaza in General Santos City in the Philippines on Monday.

item.title: Israel espía a sus aliados: el Pentágono eleva la amenaza al nivel crítico
item.description: Mientras combaten juntos contra Irán, Estados Unidos descubrió que Israel intensificó su espionaje sobre los principales negociadores del gobierno de Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: l presidente Donald Trump y el primer ministro Benjamín Netanyahu en diciembre pasado. Estados Unidos e Israel estuvieron ampliamente alineados al inicio de la guerra, y Trump respaldó el objetivo de Netanyahu de derrocar al gobierno iraní.

item.title: ‘Ragtime’ Wins the Tony for Best Revival of a Musical
item.description: Based on the novel by E.L. Doctorow, “Ragtime” follows three communities colliding in turn-of-the-20th-century New York.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Green
item.category: Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:description: Nichelle Lewis, left, as Sarah and Joshua Henry as Coalhouse Walker Jr. in the musical “Ragtime.”

item.title: Oil Surges After Hostilities Between Iran and Israel Escalate
item.description: Oil prices jumped and stocks plunged after Iran and Israel exchanged strikes, raising doubts about the future of fragile cease-fire in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Maps: 7.8-Magnitude Earthquake Near the Philippines Raises Tsunami Alerts
item.description: View the location of the quake’s epicenter and shake area.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee and Bea Malsky

item.title: ‘Liberation’ Wins the Tony for Best Play
item.description: The playwright Bess Wohl is the first American woman to win this award since Wendy Wasserstein won for “The Heidi Chronicles” in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Alexis Soloski

media:content.media:description: From left: Audrey Corsa, Susannah Flood, Irene Sofia Lucio and Adina Verson in “Liberation.”

item.title: Nithya Raman Overtakes Spencer Pratt in Race for L.A. Mayor
item.description: Nithya Raman pulled into second place in the race to see who will face Mayor Karen Bass in November. There are more votes to be counted.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler

media:content.media:description: Nithya Raman campaigns in downtown Los Angeles ahead of the mayoral primary last month.

item.title: John Lithgow Wins the Tony for Best Actor in a Play
item.description: In “Giant,” Lithgow leans into monstrosity to play the famed author Roald Dahl.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Sherman

media:content.media:description: John Lithgow as Roald Dahl in “Giant” at the Music Box Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: A Landmark Tonys Win for the Trans Costume Designer Qween Jean
item.description: Qween Jean won for “Cats: The Jellicle Ball,” becoming the first openly transgender person to win a Tony Award, according to a publicist for the show.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

media:content.media:description: Qween Jean accepting the Tony Award for best costume design of a musical, for “Cats: The Jellicle Ball.”

item.title: La princesa Mette-Marit de Noruega espera un trasplante de pulmón
item.description: La noticia del empeoramiento del estado de salud de la princesa se produce cuando una serie de escándalos ha sumido a la familia real en una profunda crisis.
item.dc:creator: Henrik Pryser Libell and Jonathan Wolfe

media:content.media:credit: Lise Åserud/NTB Scanpix, vía Associated Press
media:content.media:description: La princesa heredera Mette-Marit de Noruega, en el centro, con el príncipe heredero Haakon, a la derecha, y el príncipe Sverre Magnus en las celebraciones del Día Nacional en Asker, Noruega, el mes pasado.

item.title: Pink Is Hosting Tonight’s Tony Awards
item.description: The pop star, a former theater kid, will also perform on Sunday. Her theater-loving daughter approves of the gig.
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik

media:content.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Pink arrives at the 79th Tony Awards before hosting the ceremony.

item.title: Huge Runaway Flag Knocks Out Power to Thousands of Connecticut Customers
item.description: The flag flew into transmission lines on Saturday night, affecting 5,000 customers. In a separate episode on Sunday, 40,000 customers lost power, according to Eversource Energy.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz

media:content.media:description: An American flag on a utility tower in Stamford, Conn., on Saturday. Thousands of customers lost power over the weekend as a result.

item.title: Iran Fires Missiles at Israel for First Time Since April
item.description: Iran attacked Israel with ballistic missiles late Sunday after an Israeli attack in the outskirts of Beirut against Hezbollah, the Iranian-backed militant group.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: A projectile in the skies near Hebron, Israel, on Sunday.

item.title: Tony Award Winners 2026: See the Full List
item.description: The Tony Awards were held at Radio City Music Hall in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Sherman

media:content.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: John Lithgow, the star of “Giant,” with his award for best actor in a play.

item.title: Our Favorite Photos From the Tony Awards
item.description: As Broadway’s brightest gathered for the industry’s biggest night, here’s an up-close look at what you couldn’t see on TV.
item.dc:creator: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet

media:content.media:description: Lesley Manville after she won for her performance in “Oedipus” on Sunday night.

item.title: Robert Coles, Pulitzer-Winning Child Psychiatrist, Is Dead at 97
item.description: His five-volume “Children of Crisis” series, published between 1967 and 1977, drew on his conversations with American children whose voices were not often heard.
item.dc:creator: Douglas Martin

media:content.media:credit: Jodi Hilton for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Robert Coles eschewed ideologies and psychiatric orthodoxies to visit the homes of children — first in the American South and then around the world — to listen intently to what they had to say.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 8, 2026
item.description: Tom McCoy’s crossword is more than standard fare.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tangled up and blue? Perk up with the Wordplay column for hints and spoilers to help you finish solving.

item.title: Tony Awards 2026 Red Carpet: See the Looks of Broadway’s Biggest Stars
item.description: Here’s what Broadway’s biggest names wore to walk the blue carpet at the 79th Tony Awards.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk

media:content.media:description: Jeremy Pope, in Robert Wun, making an entrance that can only be described as “theatrical.”

item.title: Tonys 2026 Highlights: ‘Schmigadoon!’ and ‘Death of a Salesman’ Are Big Winners
item.description: “Ragtime” won best musical revival, while “Death of a Salesman” led with six prizes. Joshua Henry, Caissie Levy, John Lithgow and Laurie Metcalf were among the winners.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
